records.ety: [OE. breden, AS. brēdan to nourish, cherish, keep warm, from brōd brood; akin to D. broeden to brood, OHG. bruoten, G. brüten. See Brood.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Bred (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Breeding.]

defs: To produce as offspring; to bring forth; to bear; to procreate; to generate; to beget; to hatch.
quotes: Yet every mother breeds not sons alike.
If the sun breed maggots in a dead dog.

defs: To take care of in infancy, and through the age of youth; to bring up; to nurse and foster.
quotes: To bring thee forth with pain, with care to breed.
Born and bred on the verge of the wilderness.

defs: To educate; to instruct; to form by education; to train; -- sometimes followed by up.
quotes: But no care was taken to breed him a Protestant.
His farm may not remove his children too far from him, or the trade he breeds them up in.

defs: To engender; to cause; to occasion; to originate; to produce; as, to breed a storm; to breed disease.
quotes: Lest the place And my quaint habits breed astonishment.

defs: To give birth to; to be the native place of; as, a pond breeds fish; a northern country breeds stout men.
To raise, as any kind of stock.
To produce or obtain by any natural process.
quotes: Children would breed their teeth with less danger.

defs: To bear and nourish young; to reproduce or multiply itself; to be pregnant.
quotes: That they breed abundantly in the earth.
The mother had never bred before.
Ant. Is your gold and silver ewes and rams? Shy. I can not tell. I make it breed as fast.

defs: To be formed in the parent or dam; to be generated, or to grow, as young before birth.
To have birth; to be produced or multiplied.
quotes: Heavens rain grace On that which breeds between them.

defs: To raise a breed; to get progeny.
quotes: The kind of animal which you wish to breed from.

defs: A race or variety of men or other animals (or of plants), perpetuating its special or distinctive characteristics by inheritance.
quotes: Twice fifteen thousand hearts of England's breed.

defs: Class; sort; kind; -- of men, things, or qualities.
quotes: Are these the breed of wits so wondered at?
This courtesy is not of the right breed.

defs: A number produced at once; a brood.
notes: ☞ Breed is usually applied to domestic animals; species or variety to wild animals and to plants; and race to men.
